title: [Feature]: Add optional NATS no-echo connection setting
problem: NATS clients can use a no-echo setting in some publish and subscribe workflows. nats-sinks primarily consumes from JetStream pull consumers and publishes only to DLQ when configured, so the practical value of no-echo needs evaluation before adding configuration surface.
proposal: Expose `nats.no_echo` as a validated boolean configuration option, pass it to `nats-py` as `no_echo`, document when same-connection echo suppression matters, and keep the default disabled so existing sink deployments are unchanged.
users: NATS operators with specialized connection policy requirements and maintainers tracking compatibility options.
delivery_semantics: No-echo must not affect commit-then-ACK ordering, DLQ publication, or pull-consumer behavior. If the setting is irrelevant for the runner, documentation should say so clearly.
security: The setting should not expose secrets or widen permissions. Configuration must remain explicit and redacted in effective output.
acceptance: The project documents whether no-echo is supported or intentionally unnecessary.
If implemented, the config field is validated and tested.
NATS option construction tests cover the setting.
tests: Add configuration and NATS option construction tests. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/configuration.md, docs/operations.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after release publication or documented decision. Include release tag or decision, tests, and limitations.
